body:
`ssot-core` SHALL support two authored content source modes for ADR and SPEC document mutations:

- inline body text via `body`
- authored payload file via `body_file`

For `create_document()`:
- exactly one of `body` or `body_file` SHALL be provided
- providing neither SHALL fail validation
- providing both SHALL fail validation

For `update_document()`:
- at most one of `body` or `body_file` MAY be provided
- providing both SHALL fail validation
- providing neither SHALL preserve the existing authored body while allowing other mutable fields to change

When `body` is provided, `ssot-core` SHALL normalize it into the same canonical persisted ADR or SPEC document shape used by file-based authored payloads.

`body_file` validation rules remain independent of inline body support. If a file-based payload is supplied, it SHALL still satisfy the authored document payload contract for that document kind.

This contract applies to:
- ADR creation and update
- SPEC creation and update
summary: `ssot-core` SHALL support two authored content source modes for ADR and SPEC document mutations:
title: Authored document input modes
